# FlowGram.AI

> FlowGram.AI

## Guide

- [Introduction](/en/guide/getting-started/introduction.md): FlowGram is a workflow development framework and toolkit. It helps developers build AI workflow platforms faster and more easily.
FlowGram comes with a suite of built-in tools for workflow development: visual flow canvas, node configuration forms, variable scope chain, and ready-to-use materials.
Use FlowGram to build your own AI workflow platform.
- [Quick Start](/en/guide/getting-started/quick-start.md): import {
PackageManagerTabs
// @ts-ignore
} from '@theme'; :::info
To quickly experience FlowGram.AI, you can directly open it in CodeSandbox or open it in StackBlitz.
::: Choose a way to start: Option 1: Use the official template scaffolding to build a new project (⭐️ Recommended for a quick start).Option 2: Integrate into an existing project by installing the editor package.
- [Free Layout](/en/guide/getting-started/free-layout.md): import {
PackageManagerTabs
// @ts-ignore
} from '@theme';
- [Fixed Layout](/en/guide/getting-started/fixed-layout.md): import {
PackageManagerTabs
// @ts-ignore
} from '@theme';
- [Loading and Saving](/en/guide/free-layout/load.md): Canvas data is stored through WorkflowDocument
- [Nodes](/en/guide/free-layout/node.md): Nodes are defined through FlowNodeEntity
- [Lines](/en/guide/free-layout/line.md): WorkflowLinesManager manages all linesWorkflowNodeLinesData manages lines connected to nodesWorkflowLineEntity line entity
- [Ports](/en/guide/free-layout/port.md): WorkflowNodePortsData manages all port information for nodesWorkflowPortEntity port instanceWorkflowPortRender port rendering component
- [Sub-canvas](/en/guide/free-layout/sub-canvas.md): For detailed code, see Free Layout Best Practices
- [Loading and Saving](/en/guide/fixed-layout/load.md): Canvas data is stored through FlowDocument
- [Nodes](/en/guide/fixed-layout/node.md): Nodes are defined through FlowNodeEntity
- [Composite nodes](/en/guide/fixed-layout/composite-nodes.md): Composite nodes are composed of multiple nodes and support custom lines, such as condition, loop, and TryCatch.
- [Node Form](/en/guide/form/form.md)
- [Without Form](/en/guide/form/without-form.md): When the node engine is disabled, the node's data will be stored in node.getExtInfo, as shown below
- [Official Form Materials](/en/guide/form/form-materials.md): Transferred to Introduction
- [Introduction](/en/guide/variable/basic.md): What is Variable? And why Variable Engine needed?
- [Output Variables](/en/guide/variable/variable-output.md): Introduction to using FlowGram's variable engine to output variables
- [Consuming Variables](/en/guide/variable/variable-consume.md): Introduction to consuming variables output by FlowGram's variable engine
- [Concepts](/en/guide/variable/concept.md): Introduces the core concepts of the variable engine.
- [Scope Chain](/en/guide/variable/custom-scope-chain.md): How to customize the scope chain
- [@flowgram.ai/background-plugin](/en/guide/plugin/background-plugin.md): The background plugin is used to customize canvas background effects, supporting dot patterns, logo display, and neumorphism visual effects.
- [@flowgram.ai/minimap-plugin](/en/guide/plugin/minimap-plugin.md): <PackageManagerTabs command={{
npm: "npm install @flowgram.ai/minimap-plugin"
}} />
- [@flowgram.ai/export-plugin](/en/guide/plugin/export-plugin.md): <PackageManagerTabs command={{
npm: "npm install @flowgram.ai/export-plugin"
}} /> The export plugin provides functionality to export workflows as images (PNG, JPEG, SVG) or data files (JSON, YAML).
- [@flowgram.ai/panel-manager-plugin](/en/guide/plugin/panel-manager-plugin.md): A plugin for managing different types of panels.
- [@flowgram.ai/free-auto-layout-plugin](/en/guide/plugin/free-auto-layout-plugin.md): An automatic layout plugin based on the Dagre algorithm that provides intelligent node arrangement functionality for free layout canvases.
- [@flowgram.ai/free-stack-plugin](/en/guide/plugin/free-stack-plugin.md): A layer management plugin that provides z-index layer control functionality for nodes and connections in free layout canvas.
- [Scroll And Zoom](/en/guide/advanced/zoom-scroll.md): > See Playground
- [History](/en/guide/advanced/history.md): Undo/Redo is a plugin of FlowGram.AI, which is provided in both @flowgram.ai/fixed-layout-editor and @flowgram.ai/free-layout-editor.
- [Shortcuts](/en/guide/advanced/shortcuts.md)
- [Custom Service](/en/guide/advanced/custom-service.md): In business, it is necessary to abstract singleton services for easy plug-in management.
- [Custom Layer](/en/guide/advanced/custom-layer.md): We split the canvas into multiple Layers, implementing the concept of interaction layering for better plugin management. For more details, see Canvas Engine Use observeEntityDatas, observeEntities, and observeEntity to monitor updates to any data module of canvas nodesUse onZoom, onScroll, onViewportChange, etc. to monitor canvas zooming or scrollingUse render to insert React elements into the canvas, such as drawing SVG lines
- [Custom Plugin](/en/guide/advanced/custom-plugin.md)
- [概念](/en/guide/concepts/index.md): CanvasEngine: Canvas engine is responsible for drawing the "point-line" diagram, ensuring the smoothness of large-scale nodesNodeEngine: Node engine provides rendering, verification, data modification, etc. form capabilitiesVariableEngine: Variable engine introduces a scope model, abstracts variables in various business scenariosMaterial: Material library includes default ICONs, etc. UI, and can be extended after business access
- [Canvas Engine](/en/guide/concepts/canvas-engine.md)
- [Node Engine](/en/guide/concepts/node-engine.md): The Node Engine is a framework for writing the logic of process nodes. It allows businesses to focus on their own rendering and data logic without having to worry about the underlying APIs of the canvas and the interaction between nodes. At the same time, the Node Engine has precipitated the best practices for writing nodes, which helps businesses solve various problems that may arise in process - related business, such as the coupling of data logic and rendering. The Node Engine is optional. If you don't have the following complex node logic, you can choose not to enable the Node Engine and maintain node data and rendering on your own. Examples of complex node logic include: 1) The ability to validate or trigger data side - effects even when nodes are not rendered; 2) Rich interaction between nodes; 3) Redo/undo functionality; and so on.
- [ECS](/en/guide/concepts/ecs.md)
- [IOC](/en/guide/concepts/ioc.md)
- [Comparison with ReactFlow](/en/guide/concepts/reactflow.md): Reactflow is an excellent open-source project with clear architecture and code. However, it focuses on low-level flow rendering engine architecture (Node, Edge, Handle), requiring extensive development at the upper layer to adapt to complex scenarios (such as fixed layouts, which need data modeling and layout algorithms). Advanced features are paid. Compared to ReactFlow, FlowGram aims to provide a complete out-of-the-box solution for flow editing. Below are the pro paid features officially provided by ReactFlow ReactFlow events are bound to atomized DOM nodes and built-in, making interaction customization costly. Deep development requires understanding its source code. For example, it's difficult to select points when the canvas is zoomed out:
- [Introduction](/en/guide/runtime/introduction.md): Basic concepts and design principles of FlowGram Runtime
- [Quick Start](/en/guide/runtime/quick-start.md): Get started quickly with FlowGram Runtime
- [Schema](/en/guide/runtime/schema.md): Detailed introduction to FlowGram Schema structure and configuration
- [Nodes](/en/guide/runtime/node.md): This document provides a detailed introduction to the node system in FlowGram Runtime, including basic node concepts, existing node types and their usage, and how to create custom nodes. Existing Nodes: Start NodeEnd NodeLLM NodeCondition NodeLoop Node Future support will include Code, Intent, Batch, Break, Continue, and HTTP nodes
- [API](/en/guide/runtime/api.md): FlowGram Runtime API
- [Source Code Guide](/en/guide/runtime/source-code-guide.md): Analysis of FlowGram Runtime source code structure and implementation details
- [Contribution Guide](/en/guide/contributing.md): This document helps you quickly develop, test, and submit PRs in this repository. The repository uses a Monorepo managed by Rush + pnpm, and the documentation site is built with Rspress.
- [Contact US](/en/guide/contact-us.md): Issues: IssuesDiscord: https://discord.gg/SwDWdrgA9f
- [Free Layout Lines](/en/guide/advanced/lines.md): The lines in the free layout are managed by WorkflowLinesManager.

## Materials

- [Getting Started](/en/materials/introduction.md)
- [Material Management CLI](/en/materials/cli.md): Flowgram provides a dedicated CLI command-line tool to help you manage official materials in your project.
- [TypeSelector](/en/materials/components/type-selector.md): TypeSelector is a type selector component used for selecting JSON Schema types in forms. It supports both basic types and composite types (such as array types).
- [JsonSchemaEditor](/en/materials/components/json-schema-editor.md): JsonSchemaEditor is a visual JSON Schema editor that supports creating and editing complex JSON Schema structures. It provides a tree-structured interface to intuitively define properties of various types such as objects, arrays, strings, and numbers, supporting nested structures and required field marking. You can learn about the JsonSchema protocol through the following documentation: Json Schema Official SiteJson Schema Specification
- [JsonSchemaCreator](/en/materials/components/json-schema-creator.md): JsonSchemaCreator is a component that automatically generates JSON Schema from JSON strings. It provides a button to trigger a modal where users can paste JSON data, and the component automatically analyzes the data structure and generates the corresponding JSON Schema.
- [VariableSelector](/en/materials/components/variable-selector.md): VariableSelector is a component for selecting variables in the current scope, with filtering capabilities based on variable types. :::warning In the variable tree of VariableSelector, every leaf node and non-leaf node is a variable. In the official material library design, each node outputs an ObjectType variable declaration where: The metadata of the variable includes the node's title and node's Icon, which is parsed and displayed by VariableSelectorThe drill-down fields of the variable are recursively displayed within the node variable by VariableSelector :::
- [DynamicValueInput](/en/materials/components/dynamic-value-input.md): DynamicValueInput is a dynamic value input component that supports both constant and variable input modes. It can automatically select the appropriate input type based on the provided schema and offers variable selection functionality. The component can intelligently switch between constant input and variable selection.
- [ConditionRow](/en/materials/components/condition-row.md): ConditionRow is a conditional expression component used to build variable comparison logic. It supports selecting variables, choosing comparison operators, and inputting comparison values. It can automatically adapt available operators and value types based on the variable type.
- [DBConditionRow](/en/materials/components/db-condition-row.md): DBConditionRow is a database condition row component used for building database query conditions. It provides field selection, operator selection, and value input functionality, which can automatically display appropriate operators and input controls based on field types.
- [ConditionContext](/en/materials/components/condition-context.md): ConditionContext is a context management system for condition configuration, used to uniformly manage condition rules and operator configurations, providing a consistent configuration environment for condition components. :::tip The condition configuration context of ConditionContext can affect the following materials: ConditionRowDBConditionRow :::
- [InputsValues](/en/materials/components/inputs-values.md): InputsValues is a key-value pair input list component used to collect and manage a set of input parameters. Each key-value pair supports both constant and variable input modes, implemented through the DynamicValueInput component for flexible input methods.
- [InputsValuesTree](/en/materials/components/inputs-values-tree.md): InputsValuesTree is a component for displaying and editing tree-structured input values. Each leaf node is a key-value pair, with values supporting both constant and variable input modes through the DynamicValueInput component. The component uses a tree hierarchy display with expand/collapse functionality, making it suitable for building complex nested data structures. :::tip{title="Difference from InputsValues"} Structure Difference: InputsValues only supports flat key-value pairs, while InputsValuesTree supports tree-structured nested dataDisplay Method: InputsValues uses a simple list display, while InputsValuesTree uses a tree structure with indentation and expand/collapse functionalityUsage Scenarios: InputsValues is suitable for simple key-value pair configurations, while InputsValuesTree is suitable for complex multi-level data structure configurations :::
- [PromptEditor](/en/materials/components/prompt-editor.md): PromptEditor is a professional prompt editor component built on Coze Editor, supporting Markdown syntax highlighting, Jinja template syntax highlighting, and other features, suitable for creating and editing AI prompt templates.
- [PromptEditorWithVariables](/en/materials/components/prompt-editor-with-variables.md): PromptEditorWithVariables is an enhanced prompt editor that integrates variable management functionality. Built on PromptEditor, it provides a variable tree selector and variable tag injection, enabling users to conveniently reference and manage variables in prompt templates.
- [PromptEditorWithInputs](/en/materials/components/prompt-editor-with-inputs.md): PromptEditorWithInputs is an enhanced prompt editor that integrates input variable management functionality. Built on PromptEditor, it additionally provides a tree-structured variable selector for inputs, enabling users to conveniently reference and manage input variables in prompt templates.
- [CodeEditor](/en/materials/components/code-editor.md): CodeEditor is a powerful code editor component built on CodeMirror 6, supporting syntax highlighting and intelligent suggestions for multiple programming languages. It provides dedicated editor versions for TypeScript, Python, SQL, Shell, JSON, and more.
- [JsonEditorWithVariables](/en/materials/components/json-editor-with-variables.md): JsonEditorWithVariables is an enhanced JSON editor that supports inserting variable references into JSON. Built on JsonCodeEditor, it integrates variable selectors and variable tag injection functionality, allowing users to reference variables in JSON strings using the {{variable}} syntax.
- [SQLEditorWithVariables](/en/materials/components/sql-editor-with-variables.md): SQLEditorWithVariables is an enhanced SQL editor that supports inserting variable references in SQL. It is built on top of SQLCodeEditor and integrates a variable selector and variable tag injection, allowing users to reference variables using the {{variable}} syntax in SQL strings.
- [CozeEditorExtensions](/en/materials/components/coze-editor-extensions.md): CozeEditorExtensions is a set of functional extensions based on coze-editor, providing variable selection, Inputs selector, and variable tag echo capabilities. EditorVariableTree: Monitors trigger characters like @/{, pops up an available variable tree, and writes the selected item into the editor.EditorVariableTagInject: Renders {{variable.path}} text with markup, displaying variable icons, titles, and echo hints.EditorInputsTree: Constructs a hierarchical tree based on node inputsValues, supporting insertion of {{inputs.xxx}} references in prompts.
- [ConstantInput](/en/materials/components/constant-input.md): ConstantInput is a constant input component that automatically selects the appropriate input type renderer based on the provided JSON Schema. The component supports custom rendering strategies and fallback renderers, capable of handling constant inputs for various data types.
- [DisplaySchemaTag](/en/materials/components/display-schema-tag.md): DisplaySchemaTag is a tag component used to display JSON Schema. When users click on the tag, a detailed schema tree structure will pop up, making it easy to view complex data structures.
- [DisplaySchemaTree](/en/materials/components/display-schema-tree.md): DisplaySchemaTree is a tree component used to visually display JSON Schema structure, which can recursively show hierarchical relationships of complex data structures such as objects and arrays.
- [DisplayFlowValue](/en/materials/components/display-flow-value.md): DisplayFlowValue is a component used to visually display Flow Value data types. It can infer the corresponding JSON Schema based on the type of Flow Value and present it to users through a user-friendly interface.
- [DisplayInputsValues](/en/materials/components/display-inputs-values.md): DisplayInputsValues is a component used to visually display tree-structured input values. It can recursively traverse input value objects, parse the types of Flow Values within them, and derive the JSON Schema structure for each field. :::tip DisplayInputsValues supports displaying values configured by both InputsValues and InputsValuesTree components. :::
- [DisplayOutputs](/en/materials/components/display-outputs.md): DisplayOutputs is a component for visually displaying node output variables, supporting automatic retrieval of output variables from scope or manual specification of output schema through field values.
- [AssignRow](/en/materials/components/assign-row.md): AssignRow is an assignment row component that supports two operation modes: assignment mode (assign) and declaration mode (declare). In assignment mode: the left side is a variable selector, and the right side is dynamic value input;In declaration mode: the left side is a text input box, and the right side is dynamic value input.
- [AssignRows](/en/materials/components/assign-rows.md): AssignRows is an assignment row list component implemented based on FieldArray, supporting dynamic addition and deletion of assignment rows. The component provides two action buttons: Assign and Declare, which can add assignment mode and declaration mode assignment rows respectively. Each assignment row can be configured and deleted independently. :::tip AssignRows is typically used together with the infer-assign-plugin form plugin to convert defined declarations into node output variables and achieve automatic type linkage. :::
- [BatchOutputs (WIP)](/en/materials/components/batch-outputs.md): <SourceCode
href="https://github.com/bytedance/flowgram.ai/tree/main/packages/materials/form-materials/src/components/batch-outputs"
/>
- [BatchVariableSelector](/en/materials/components/batch-variable-selector.md): BatchVariableSelector is a component for selecting array-type variables. It's a wrapper around VariableSelector that automatically filters the variable tree to show only array-type variables and provides private scope support. It's commonly used in batch processing scenarios (such as selecting loop data sources in Loop nodes). Core Features: 🔍 Auto Filtering: Only displays array-type (type: 'array') variables🔐 Private Scope: Provides isolated variable scope through PrivateScopeProvider🎯 Specialized Scenarios: Designed for batch processing, loops, and other scenarios requiring array data sources📦 Ready to Use: No need to manually configure schema filtering conditions, automatically applies array type constraints
- [BlurInput](/en/materials/components/blur-input.md): BlurInput is a special input component that only triggers value updates when the input loses focus, suitable for scenarios where frequent update operations need to be avoided.
- [autoRenameRef](/en/materials/effects/auto-rename-ref.md): autoRenameRef is an automatic reference renaming effect that automatically updates all reference values and template values referencing that field when the key name of a form field changes.
- [listenRefSchemaChange](/en/materials/effects/listen-ref-schema-change.md): Listen to JSON schema changes of reference variable types and trigger callback functions when changes occur.
- [listenRefValueChange](/en/materials/effects/listen-ref-value-change.md): Listen for changes for the referenced variable definition and trigger a callback function when it changes.
- [provideBatchInput (WIP)](/en/materials/effects/provide-batch-input.md): <SourceCode
href="https://github.com/bytedance/flowgram.ai/tree/main/packages/materials/form-materials/src/effects/provide-batch-input"
/>
- [provideJsonSchemaOutputs](/en/materials/effects/provide-json-schema-outputs.md): provideJsonSchemaOutputs is a form effect that converts JSON Schema definitions into output variables in the FlowGram variable engine. It automatically parses the JSON Schema structure defined in the form into variable declarations, allowing other nodes in the workflow to reference these outputs.
- [syncVariableTitle](/en/materials/effects/sync-variable-title.md): syncVariableTitle is a form effect used to synchronize the node's title to the metadata of its output variables. When the node title changes, it automatically updates the title and icon metadata of all output variables.
- [validateWhenVariableSync](/en/materials/effects/validate-when-variable-sync.md): When accessible variables of a field change, re-trigger validation for the specified error fields. :::note{title="Why error fields?"} Error messages for error fields may be derived from the validity of variable references. If the accessible variables of a field change, making the variable references of the field change from invalid to valid, there's a need to re-validate the current field to clear previous error messages. :::
- [batchOutputsPlugin (WIP)](/en/materials/form-plugins/batch-outputs-plugin.md): <SourceCode
href="https://github.com/bytedance/flowgram.ai/tree/main/packages/materials/form-materials/src/form-plugins/batch-outputs-plugin"
/>
- [inferAssignPlugin](/en/materials/form-plugins/infer-assign-plugin.md): inferAssignPlugin is a form plugin used for variable assignment nodes to automatically derive output variables. It is typically used in conjunction with the AssignRows component. The plugin implements the following capabilities for declare (declaring new variables) in AssignRows: Automatically generates node output variables, where the variable name is the left-hand value in the declare operator, and the variable type is automatically inferred based on the right value.When submitting data to the backend, automatically generates the corresponding JSON Schema based on the output variable types.
- [inferInputsPlugin](/en/materials/form-plugins/infer-inputs-plugin.md): inferInputsPlugin is a form plugin for automatically inferring JSON Schema of input parameters. It can automatically generate corresponding JSON Schema structures based on IFlowValue types (constants or variable references) before data is sent to the backend runtime, providing type information for backend runtime type validation and backend interfaces. :::tip{title="Applicable Scenarios"} HTTP Nodes: Infer Schema for request headers and query parametersCode Nodes: Infer type structure for code input parametersFunction Call Nodes: Infer Schema for function parametersAny nodes accepting dynamic inputs: Need to provide input type information for the backend :::
- [validateFlowValue](/en/materials/validate/validate-flow-value.md): validateFlowValue is a validation function for verifying the requiredness and variable reference validity of FlowValue.
- [FlowValue](/en/materials/common/flow-value.md): FlowValue is a special type used in Flowgram Official Materials to represent data. It can be a constant, reference, expression, or template, providing a flexible way for data transfer and processing in workflows.
- [Type Management](/en/materials/common/json-schema-preset.md): Type management is implemented in two parts: Material Layer (preset type definitions in the material library):Extends the type engine to define default renderers, condition rule configurations, etc. for typesProvides preset definitions for default types in the material library (constant input renderer, condition rule configuration, etc.)Provides Editor Plugin for easily extending custom typesEngine Layer (core type engine, provided by @flowgram.ai/json-schema)Provides basic definitions of Json types, including icons, name display, etc.Provides BaseTypeManager, which can extend type definitions beyond Json SchemaProvides JsonSchemaUtils to implement mutual conversion between JSONSchema and AST
- [Material Dependency Injection](/en/materials/common/inject-material.md): :::tip{title="Component Materials Supporting Dependency Injection in the Material Library"} InjectDynamicValueInputInjectTypeSelectorInjectVariableSelector :::
- [DisableDeclarationPlugin](/en/materials/common/disable-declaration-plugin.md): :::note{title=""} In the design of the materials library, "nodes themselves" are defined as VariableDeclaration. Components in the materials library such as VariableSelector, PromptEditorWithVariables, and SQLEditorWithVariables all support selecting "node" by default. ::: DisableDeclarationPlugin can disable variable declarations (only allowing drilling down) , making "node" unselectable.

## Examples

- [](/en/examples/index.md)
- [PlaygroundReact](/en/examples/playground.md): PlaygroundReact is the underlying module of fixed-layout and free-layout, which can be used separately, Features: Infinite drag on the canvasMouse or touchpad gesture zoomingBuilt-in background pluginBuilt-in shortcut plugin
- [Basic Usage](/en/examples/fixed-layout/fixed-layout-simple.md)
- [Composite Nodes](/en/examples/fixed-layout/fixed-composite-nodes.md)
- [Best Practices](/en/examples/fixed-layout/fixed-feature-overview.md)
- [Basic Usage](/en/examples/free-layout/free-layout-simple.md)
- [Best Practices](/en/examples/free-layout/free-feature-overview.md)
- [Basic Usage](/en/examples/node-form/basic.md)
- [Effect](/en/examples/node-form/effect.md): The following examples demonstrate how to configure form side effects. Two examples are provided, with behaviors described below: Basic effect: When a form field value changes, the current form values will be printed to the console.Control other fields: When the current form field data changes, it will simultaneously change the value of another form field.
- [Array](/en/examples/node-form/array.md): The following example demonstrates the basic usage of arrays, including: Basic implementation (rendering, adding and removing items).How to configure validation logic for each array item. In this case, the validation rule is that each item should not exceed 8 English characters.How to configure side effects for each array item. Here, the side effect outputs ${name} value init to ${value} to the console during initialization, and ${name} value changed to ${value} when the value changes.How to swap array items.
- [Dynamic Field](/en/examples/node-form/dynamic.md): This example demonstrates how to declare dependencies between form fields using the deps property. Example explanation: The City field will only be displayed when Country has a value. You can also use form.getValueIn('country') as an input parameter for the component under the city Field to control the component's behavior, such as filtering cities based on the selected country.

## API

- [](/en/api/index.md)
- [FlowDocument](/en/api/core/flow-document.md): Flow document (fixed layout), stores all node data of the process > API Detail :::danger
The best way to operate nodes is through ctx.operation, so that it can be bound to redo/undo
:::
- [FlowNodeEntity/WorkflowNodeEntity](/en/api/core/flow-node-entity.md): Node entity, WorkflowNodeEntity is the alias for the node used for free layout nodes, the node entity uses the ECS architecture, is Entity > API Detail
- [WorkflowDocument (free)](/en/api/core/workflow-document.md): Free layout document data, inherited from FlowDocument > API Detail :::tip
Due to historical reasons, all names with the Workflow prefix represent free layout
:::
- [WorkflowLinesManager (free)](/en/api/core/workflow-lines-manager.md): Free layout line management, currently attached to the free layout document > API Detail
- [WorkflowLineEntity (free)](/en/api/core/workflow-line-entity.md): Free layout line entity > API Detail
- [Playground](/en/api/core/playground.md): Canvas instance > API Detail
- [useClientContext](/en/api/hooks/use-client-context.md): Provides access to the canvas context within React. Currently, there are some differences between fixed layout and free layout.
- [useNodeRender](/en/api/hooks/use-node-render.md): Provides methods related to node rendering, and the form returned is equivalent to getNodeForm
- [usePlaygroundTools](/en/api/hooks/use-playground-tools.md): Canvas tool methods
- [useRefresh](/en/api/hooks/use-refresh.md)
- [useService](/en/api/hooks/use-service.md): Get all singleton modules of the underlying IOC
- [EditorRenderer](/en/api/components/editor-renderer.md): Canvas rendering component, needs to be used with FixedLayoutEditorProvider or FreeLayoutEditorProvider
- [FixedLayoutEditorProvider](/en/api/components/fixed-layout-editor-provider.md): Fixed layout canvas configuration, supports ref
- [FixedLayoutEditor](/en/api/components/fixed-layout-editor.md): Fixed layout canvas, equivalent to the combination of FixedLayoutEditorProvider and EditorRenderer
- [FreeLayoutEditorProvider](/en/api/components/free-layout-editor-provider.md): Free layout canvas configuration, supports ref
- [FreeLayoutEditor](/en/api/components/free-layout-editor.md): Free layout canvas, equivalent to the combination of FreeLayoutEditorProvider and EditorRenderer
- [WorkflowNodeRenderer(free)](/en/api/components/workflow-node-renderer.md): Free layout node container
- [ClipboardService](/en/api/services/clipboard-service.md): Clipboard Service > API Detail
- [CommandService](/en/api/services/command-service.md): Command Service, needs to be used with Shortcuts > API Detail
- [FlowOperationService](/en/api/services/flow-operation-service.md): Node operation service, currently used for fixed layout, free layout can currently be operated directly through WorkflowDocument, and will be abstracted out as operation in the future > API Detail
- [](/en/api/services/history-service.md)
- [SelectionService](/en/api/services/selection-service.md): Used to control selected nodes > API Detail
- [DisposableCollection](/en/api/utils/disposable-collection.md)
- [Disposable](/en/api/utils/disposable.md)
- [Emitter](/en/api/utils/emitter.md): Event module
- [getNodeForm](/en/api/utils/get-node-form.md): Get the form capabilities of the node, needs to be enabled node engine > API Detail
- [Common APIs](/en/api/common-apis.md)
- [](/en/api/plugins.md)

- [Class: ContextMenuService](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/ContextMenuService.md): 圈选右键菜单相关 service
- [Class: DefaultClipboardService](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/DefaultClipboardService.md): 剪贴板服务，一般用于管理临时的复制黏贴数据
TODO: 后续可以支持调用浏览器
- [Class: EditorStateConfigEntity](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/EditorStateConfigEntity.md): 编辑状态管理
- [Class: Entity<OPTS>](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/Entity-1.md)
- [Class: EntityData<DATA, OPTS>](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/EntityData.md): 实体的数据块

Entity 管理器，全局唯一
- [Class: FlushLayerMessage](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/FlushLayerMessage.md)
- [Class: Layer<OPT, CONTEXT>](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/Layer.md): 基础 layer
- [Class: LocalStorageService](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/LocalStorageService.md)
- [Class: LoggerService](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/LoggerService.md): 画布全局的选择器，可以放任何东西
- [Class: OpacityData](/en/auto-docs/core/classes/OpacityData.md): 实体的数据块

- [Class: ASTNode<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ASTNode.md): An ASTNode represents a fundamental unit of variable information within the system's Abstract Syntax Tree.
It can model various constructs, for example: Declarations: const a = 1Expressions: a.b.cTypes: number, string, boolean Here is some characteristic of ASTNode: Tree-like Structure: ASTNodes can be nested to form a tree, representing complex variable structures.Extendable: New features can be added by extending the base ASTNode class.Reactive: Changes in an ASTNode's value trigger events, enabling reactive programming patterns.Serializable: ASTNodes can be converted to and from a JSON format (ASTNodeJSON) for storage or transmission.
- [Class: ASTRegisters](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ASTRegisters.md): Register the AST node to the engine.
- [Class: ArrayType](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ArrayType.md): Represents an array type.
- [Class: AsyncContainerModule](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/AsyncContainerModule.md)
- [Class: BaseExpression<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/BaseExpression.md): Base class for all expressions. All other expressions should extend this class.
- [Class: BaseType<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/BaseType.md): Base class for all types. All other types should extend this class.
- [Class: BaseVariableField<VariableMeta>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/BaseVariableField.md): Variable Field abstract class, which is the base class for VariableDeclaration and Property VariableDeclaration is used to declare a variable in a block scope.Property is used to declare a property in an object.
- [Class: BooleanType](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/BooleanType.md): Represents a boolean type.

- [Class: Circle](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/Circle.md): The Circle object is used to help draw graphics and can also be used to specify a hit area for displayObjects.

- [Class: DisposableCollection](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/DisposableCollection.md): An object that performs a cleanup operation when .dispose() is called. Some examples of how disposables are used: An event listener that removes itself when .dispose() is called.The return value from registering a provider. When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

- [Class: GlobalScope](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/GlobalScope.md): Global Scope stores all variables that are not scoped to any node. Variables in Global Scope can be accessed by any node.Any other scope's variables can not be accessed by Global Scope.

- [Class: KeyPathExpression<CustomPathJSON>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/KeyPathExpression.md): Represents a key path expression, which is used to reference a variable by its key path. This is the V2 of KeyPathExpression, with the following improvements: returnType is copied to a new instance to avoid reference issues.Circular reference detection is introduced.

- [Class: Property<VariableMeta>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/Property.md): Property is a variable field that represents a property of a ObjectType.
- [Class: ReactiveBaseState<V>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ReactiveBaseState.md)
- [Class: ReactiveState<V>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ReactiveState.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: Rectangle](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/Rectangle-1.md): Rectangle object is an area defined by its position, as indicated by its top-left corner
point (x, y) and by its width and its height.
- [Class: RotationData](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/RotationData.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: ScaleData](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ScaleData.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: Scope<ScopeMeta>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/Scope.md): Represents a variable scope, which manages its own set of variables and their lifecycle. scope.output represents the variables declared within this scope.scope.available represents all variables accessible from this scope, including those from parent scopes.
- [Class: ScopeChain](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ScopeChain.md): Manages the dependency relationships between scopes.
This is an abstract class, and specific implementations determine how the scope order is managed.
- [Class: ScopeChainTransformService](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ScopeChainTransformService.md): A service for transforming scope chains.
- [Class: ScopeOutputData](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ScopeOutputData.md): Manages the output variables of a scope.
- [Class: SelectionService](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/SelectionService.md): 画布全局的选择器，可以放任何东西
- [Class: SelectorBoxConfigEntity](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/SelectorBoxConfigEntity.md): 选择框配置
- [Class: ShortcutsRegistry](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/ShortcutsRegistry.md)
- [Class: SizeData](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/SizeData.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: SkewData](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/SkewData.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: StringType](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/StringType.md): Base class for all types. All other types should extend this class.
- [Class: Computation<V>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/Tracker.Computation.md): Tracker.Computation A Computation object represents code that is repeatedly rerun
in response to
reactive data changes. Computations don't have return values; they just
perform actions, such as rerendering a template on the screen. Computations
are created using Tracker.autorun. Use stop to prevent further rerunning of a
computation.
- [Class: Dependency](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/Tracker.Dependency.md): Tracker.Dependency A Dependency represents an atomic unit of reactive data that a
computation might depend on. Reactive data sources such as Session or
Minimongo internally create different Dependency objects for different
pieces of data, each of which may be depended on by multiple computations.
When the data changes, the computations are invalidated.
- [Class: Transform](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/Transform.md): Transform that takes care about its versions Memberof PIXI
- [Class: TransformData](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/TransformData-1.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: VariableDeclaration<VariableMeta>](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/VariableDeclaration.md): VariableDeclaration is a variable field that represents a variable declaration.
- [Class: VariableDeclarationList](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/VariableDeclarationList.md): An ASTNode represents a fundamental unit of variable information within the system's Abstract Syntax Tree.
It can model various constructs, for example: Declarations: const a = 1Expressions: a.b.cTypes: number, string, boolean Here is some characteristic of ASTNode: Tree-like Structure: ASTNodes can be nested to form a tree, representing complex variable structures.Extendable: New features can be added by extending the base ASTNode class.Reactive: Changes in an ASTNode's value trigger events, enabling reactive programming patterns.Serializable: ASTNodes can be converted to and from a JSON format (ASTNodeJSON) for storage or transmission.
- [Class: VariableEngine](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/VariableEngine.md): The core of the variable engine system.
It manages scopes, variables, and events within the system.
- [Class: VariableFieldKeyRenameService](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/VariableFieldKeyRenameService.md): This service is responsible for detecting when a variable field's key is renamed.
It listens for changes in variable declaration lists and object properties, and
determines if a change constitutes a rename operation.
- [Class: WrapArrayExpression](/en/auto-docs/editor/classes/WrapArrayExpression.md): Represents a wrap expression, which wraps an expression with an array.
- [Enumeration: ASTKind](/en/auto-docs/editor/enums/ASTKind.md): Core AST node types.

- [Function: afterFlush](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/Tracker.afterFlush.md): Tracker.afterFlush afterFlush(f): void Schedules a function to be called during the next flush, or later in the current flush if one is in progress, after all invalidated computations have been rerun.  The function will be run once and not on subsequent flushes unless afterFlush is called again. Parameters Returns void
- [Function: autorun](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/Tracker.autorun.md): Tracker.autorun autorun<T>(f, options?): Computation<T> Run a function now and rerun it later whenever its dependencies
change. Returns a Computation object that can be used to stop or observe the
rerunning. Type parameters Parameters Returns Computation<T>
- [Function: flush](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/Tracker.flush.md): Tracker.flush flush(options?): void Process all reactive updates immediately and ensure that all invalidated computations are rerun. Parameters Returns void

- [Function: inFlush](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/Tracker.inFlush.md): Tracker.inFlush inFlush(): boolean True if we are computing a computation now, either first time or recompute.  This matches Tracker.active unless we are inside Tracker.nonreactive, which nullfies currentComputation even though an enclosing computation may still be running. Returns boolean

- [Function: arrayUnion](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/arrayUnion.md): arrayUnion(arr): any[] Parameters Returns any[] See https://stackoverflow.com/a/9229821
export function arrayUnion(arr: any[]): any[] {
return [...new Set(arr)]
}

It should be used in a child component of <Field />, otherwise it throws an error Type parameters Returns IField<TFieldValue> | IFieldArray<TFieldValue>
- [Function: useCurrentFieldState](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useCurrentFieldState.md): useCurrentFieldState(): FieldState Get the current field state. It should be used in a child component of <Field />, otherwise it throws an error Returns FieldState
- [Function: useCurrentScope](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useCurrentScope.md): useCurrentScope<Strict>(params?): Strict extends true ? Scope<Record<string, any>> : undefined | Scope<Record<string, any>> useCurrentScope returns the scope provided by ScopeProvider. Type parameters Parameters Returns Strict extends true ? Scope<Record<string, any>> : undefined | Scope<Record<string, any>>
- [Function: useEntities](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useEntities.md): useEntities<T>(entityRegistry): T[] 获取 entities 并监听变化 Type parameters Parameters Returns T[] Deprecated
- [Function: useEntityDataFromContext](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useEntityDataFromContext.md): useEntityDataFromContext<T>(dataRegistry, listenChange?): T 从上下 PlaygroundEntityContext 获取 entity data 并监听变化 (默认不监听) Type parameters Parameters Returns T
- [Function: useEntityFromContext](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useEntityFromContext.md): useEntityFromContext<T>(listenChange?): T 从上下 PlaygroundEntityContext 获取 entity 并监听变化(默认不监听) Type parameters Parameters Returns T
- [Function: useField](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useField.md): useField<TFieldValue, TField>(name?): TField | undefined Type parameters Parameters Returns TField | undefined Deprecated useField is deprecated because its return relies on React render. if the Field is not rendered, the return would be
undefined. If you simply want to monitor the change of the value of a certain path, please use useWatch(fieldName)
- [Function: useFieldValidate](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useFieldValidate.md): useFieldValidate(name?): () => void Get validate method of a field with given name. the returned function could possibly do nothing if the field is not found.
The reason could be that the field is not rendered yet or the name given is wrong. Parameters Returns fn (): void Returns void
- [Function: useFlowEditor](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useFlowEditor.md): useFlowEditor(): FlowEditorClient Returns FlowEditorClient
- [Function: useForm](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useForm.md): useForm(): IForm Get Form instance. It should be use in a child component of  <Form /> Returns IForm
- [Function: useHighlight](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useHighlight.md): useHighlight(props): null | MutableRefObject<any> Parameters Returns null | MutableRefObject<any>
- [Function: useInitializedFormModel](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useInitializedFormModel.md): useInitializedFormModel(node): FormModel Listen to FormModel's initialization and refresh React component.
By providing related node, you can use this hook outside the Form Component. Parameters Returns FormModel
- [Function: useListenEvents](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useListenEvents.md): useListenEvents(...events): void 监听 event 事件变化 Parameters Returns void
- [Function: useObserve](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useObserve.md): useObserve<T>(value): T Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT Type parameters Parameters Returns T
- [Function: useOutputVariables](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useOutputVariables.md): useOutputVariables(): VariableDeclaration[] Get output variable list in the current scope. The hook is reactive to variable list or any variables change. Returns VariableDeclaration[]
- [Function: usePlayground](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/usePlayground.md): usePlayground(): Playground 获取 playground Returns Playground
- [Function: usePlaygroundContainer](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/usePlaygroundContainer.md): usePlaygroundContainer(): Container 获取 playground inversify container Returns Container

- [Function: useScopeAvailable](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useScopeAvailable.md): useScopeAvailable(params?): ScopeAvailableData Get the available variables in the current scope.
获取作用域的可访问变量 Parameters Returns ScopeAvailableData the available variables in the current scope
- [Function: useService](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useService.md): useService<T>(identifier): T 获取画布的 IOC 模块 Type parameters Parameters Returns T
- [Function: useWatch](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useWatch.md): useWatch<TValue>(name): TValue Listen to the field data change and refresh the React component. Type parameters Parameters Returns TValue
- [Function: useWatchFormErrors](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useWatchFormErrors.md): useWatchFormErrors(node): Errors | undefined Get Form's errors, Form errors is a proxy, it will refresh the React component when the value you accessed changed
By providing related node, you can use this hook outside the Form Component. Parameters Returns Errors | undefined
- [Function: useWatchFormState](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useWatchFormState.md): useWatchFormState(node): FormState | undefined Get Form's state, Form State is a proxy, it will refresh the React component when the value you accessed changed
By providing related node, you can use this hook outside the Form Component. Parameters Returns FormState | undefined
- [Function: useWatchFormValueIn](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useWatchFormValueIn.md): useWatchFormValueIn<T>(node, name): T | undefined Listen to Form's value in a certain path and refresh the React component.
By providing related node, you can use this hook outside the Form Component. Type parameters Parameters Returns T | undefined
- [Function: useWatchFormValues](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useWatchFormValues.md): useWatchFormValues<T>(node): T | undefined Listen to Form's values and refresh the React component.
By providing related node, you can use this hook outside the Form Component. Type parameters Parameters Returns T | undefined
- [Function: useWatchFormWarnings](/en/auto-docs/editor/functions/useWatchFormWarnings.md): useWatchFormWarnings(node): Warnings | undefined Get Form's warnings, Form warnings is a proxy, it will refresh the React component when the value you accessed changed
By providing related node, you can use this hook outside the Form Component. Parameters Returns Warnings | undefined

- [Type alias: ResolveRequestHandler](/en/auto-docs/editor/types/interfaces.ResolveRequestHandler.md): interfaces.ResolveRequestHandler ResolveRequestHandler: (request: Request) => unknown Type declaration (request): unknown Parameters Returns unknown
- [Type alias: ServiceIdentifier<T>](/en/auto-docs/editor/types/interfaces.ServiceIdentifier.md): interfaces.ServiceIdentifier ServiceIdentifier<T>: CommonServiceIdentifier<T> Type parameters
- [Type alias: SimpleFactory<T, U>](/en/auto-docs/editor/types/interfaces.SimpleFactory.md): interfaces.SimpleFactory SimpleFactory<T, U>: (...args: U) => T Type parameters Type declaration (...args): T Parameters Returns T
- [Type alias: Target](/en/auto-docs/editor/types/interfaces.Target.md): interfaces.Target Target: LegacyTarget
- [Type alias: TargetType](/en/auto-docs/editor/types/interfaces.TargetType.md): interfaces.TargetType TargetType: "ConstructorArgument" | "ClassProperty" | "Variable"
- [Type alias: Unbind](/en/auto-docs/editor/types/interfaces.Unbind.md): interfaces.Unbind Unbind: <T>(serviceIdentifier: ServiceIdentifier<T>) => void Type declaration <T>(serviceIdentifier): void Type parameters Parameters Returns void
- [Type alias: UnbindAsync](/en/auto-docs/editor/types/interfaces.UnbindAsync.md): interfaces.UnbindAsync UnbindAsync: <T>(serviceIdentifier: ServiceIdentifier<T>) => Promise<void> Type declaration <T>(serviceIdentifier): Promise<void> Type parameters Parameters Returns Promise<void>

- [Variable: VariableContainerModule](/en/auto-docs/editor/variables/VariableContainerModule.md): Const VariableContainerModule: ContainerModule An InversifyJS container module that binds all the necessary services for the variable engine.
This module sets up the dependency injection for the core components of the variable engine.
- [Variable: VariableEngineProvider](/en/auto-docs/editor/variables/VariableEngineProvider-1.md): Const VariableEngineProvider: unique symbol A provider for dynamically obtaining the VariableEngine instance.
This is used to prevent circular dependencies when injecting VariableEngine.
- [Variable: logger](/en/auto-docs/editor/variables/logger.md): Const logger: Logger
- [Class: ASTNode<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/ASTNode.md): An ASTNode represents a fundamental unit of variable information within the system's Abstract Syntax Tree.
It can model various constructs, for example: Declarations: const a = 1Expressions: a.b.cTypes: number, string, boolean Here is some characteristic of ASTNode: Tree-like Structure: ASTNodes can be nested to form a tree, representing complex variable structures.Extendable: New features can be added by extending the base ASTNode class.Reactive: Changes in an ASTNode's value trigger events, enabling reactive programming patterns.Serializable: ASTNodes can be converted to and from a JSON format (ASTNodeJSON) for storage or transmission.
- [Class: ASTRegisters](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/ASTRegisters.md): Register the AST node to the engine.
- [Class: ArrayType](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/ArrayType.md): Represents an array type.
- [Class: AsyncContainerModule](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/AsyncContainerModule.md)
- [Class: BaseExpression<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/BaseExpression.md): Base class for all expressions. All other expressions should extend this class.
- [Class: BaseType<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/BaseType.md): Base class for all types. All other types should extend this class.
- [Class: BaseVariableField<VariableMeta>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/BaseVariableField.md): Variable Field abstract class, which is the base class for VariableDeclaration and Property VariableDeclaration is used to declare a variable in a block scope.Property is used to declare a property in an object.
- [Class: BooleanType](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/BooleanType.md): Represents a boolean type.

- [Class: DisposableCollection](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/DisposableCollection.md): An object that performs a cleanup operation when .dispose() is called. Some examples of how disposables are used: An event listener that removes itself when .dispose() is called.The return value from registering a provider. When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

- [Class: KeyPathExpression<CustomPathJSON>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/KeyPathExpression.md): Represents a key path expression, which is used to reference a variable by its key path. This is the V2 of KeyPathExpression, with the following improvements: returnType is copied to a new instance to avoid reference issues.Circular reference detection is introduced.
- [Class: Layer<OPT, CONTEXT>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/Layer.md): 基础 layer
- [Class: LegacyKeyPathExpression<CustomPathJSON>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/LegacyKeyPathExpression.md): Deprecated Use KeyPathExpression instead.
Represents a key path expression, which is used to reference a variable by its key path.
- [Class: ListNode](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/ListNode.md): Represents a list of nodes.

Some examples of how disposables are used: An event listener that removes itself when .dispose() is called.The return value from registering a provider. When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: Scope<ScopeMeta>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/Scope.md): Represents a variable scope, which manages its own set of variables and their lifecycle. scope.output represents the variables declared within this scope.scope.available represents all variables accessible from this scope, including those from parent scopes.
- [Class: ScopeChain](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/ScopeChain.md): Manages the dependency relationships between scopes.
This is an abstract class, and specific implementations determine how the scope order is managed.
- [Class: ScopeChainTransformService](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/ScopeChainTransformService.md): A service for transforming scope chains.
- [Class: ScopeOutputData](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/ScopeOutputData.md): Manages the output variables of a scope.

SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: StringType](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/StringType.md): Base class for all types. All other types should extend this class.
- [Class: Computation<V>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/Tracker.Computation.md): Tracker.Computation A Computation object represents code that is repeatedly rerun
in response to
reactive data changes. Computations don't have return values; they just
perform actions, such as rerendering a template on the screen. Computations
are created using Tracker.autorun. Use stop to prevent further rerunning of a
computation.
- [Class: Dependency](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/Tracker.Dependency.md): Tracker.Dependency A Dependency represents an atomic unit of reactive data that a
computation might depend on. Reactive data sources such as Session or
Minimongo internally create different Dependency objects for different
pieces of data, each of which may be depended on by multiple computations.
When the data changes, the computations are invalidated.
- [Class: Transform](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/Transform.md): Transform that takes care about its versions Memberof PIXI
- [Class: TransformData](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/TransformData-1.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
- [Class: UndoRedoService](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/UndoRedoService.md): UndoRedo服务
- [Class: VariableDeclaration<VariableMeta>](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/VariableDeclaration.md): VariableDeclaration is a variable field that represents a variable declaration.
- [Class: VariableDeclarationList](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/VariableDeclarationList.md): An ASTNode represents a fundamental unit of variable information within the system's Abstract Syntax Tree.
It can model various constructs, for example: Declarations: const a = 1Expressions: a.b.cTypes: number, string, boolean Here is some characteristic of ASTNode: Tree-like Structure: ASTNodes can be nested to form a tree, representing complex variable structures.Extendable: New features can be added by extending the base ASTNode class.Reactive: Changes in an ASTNode's value trigger events, enabling reactive programming patterns.Serializable: ASTNodes can be converted to and from a JSON format (ASTNodeJSON) for storage or transmission.
- [Class: VariableEngine](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/VariableEngine.md): The core of the variable engine system.
It manages scopes, variables, and events within the system.
- [Class: VariableFieldKeyRenameService](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/VariableFieldKeyRenameService.md): This service is responsible for detecting when a variable field's key is renamed.
It listens for changes in variable declaration lists and object properties, and
determines if a change constitutes a rename operation.
- [Class: WrapArrayExpression](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/classes/WrapArrayExpression.md): Represents a wrap expression, which wraps an expression with an array.

- [Function: afterFlush](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/functions/Tracker.afterFlush.md): Tracker.afterFlush afterFlush(f): void Schedules a function to be called during the next flush, or later in the current flush if one is in progress, after all invalidated computations have been rerun.  The function will be run once and not on subsequent flushes unless afterFlush is called again. Parameters Returns void
- [Function: autorun](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/functions/Tracker.autorun.md): Tracker.autorun autorun<T>(f, options?): Computation<T> Run a function now and rerun it later whenever its dependencies
change. Returns a Computation object that can be used to stop or observe the
rerunning. Type parameters Parameters Returns Computation<T>
- [Function: flush](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/functions/Tracker.flush.md): Tracker.flush flush(options?): void Process all reactive updates immediately and ensure that all invalidated computations are rerun. Parameters Returns void

This matches Tracker.active unless we are inside Tracker.nonreactive, which nullfies currentComputation even though an enclosing computation may still be running. Returns boolean

- [Function: arrayUnion](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/functions/arrayUnion.md): arrayUnion(arr): any[] Parameters Returns any[] See https://stackoverflow.com/a/9229821
export function arrayUnion(arr: any[]): any[] {
return [...new Set(arr)]
}

undefined. If you simply want to monitor the change of the value of a certain path, please use useWatch(fieldName)

The reason could be that the field is not rendered yet or the name given is wrong. Parameters Returns fn (): void Returns void

When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

- [Variable: VariableContainerModule](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/variables/VariableContainerModule.md): Const VariableContainerModule: ContainerModule An InversifyJS container module that binds all the necessary services for the variable engine.
This module sets up the dependency injection for the core components of the variable engine.
- [Variable: VariableEngineProvider](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/variables/VariableEngineProvider-1.md): Const VariableEngineProvider: unique symbol A provider for dynamically obtaining the VariableEngine instance.
This is used to prevent circular dependencies when injecting VariableEngine.
- [Variable: logger](/en/auto-docs/fixed-layout-editor/variables/logger.md): Const logger: Logger
- [Class: ASTNode<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ASTNode.md): An ASTNode represents a fundamental unit of variable information within the system's Abstract Syntax Tree.
It can model various constructs, for example: Declarations: const a = 1Expressions: a.b.cTypes: number, string, boolean Here is some characteristic of ASTNode: Tree-like Structure: ASTNodes can be nested to form a tree, representing complex variable structures.Extendable: New features can be added by extending the base ASTNode class.Reactive: Changes in an ASTNode's value trigger events, enabling reactive programming patterns.Serializable: ASTNodes can be converted to and from a JSON format (ASTNodeJSON) for storage or transmission.
- [Class: ASTRegisters](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ASTRegisters.md): Register the AST node to the engine.
- [Class: ArrayType](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ArrayType.md): Represents an array type.
- [Class: AsyncContainerModule](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/AsyncContainerModule.md)
- [Class: BaseExpression<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/BaseExpression.md): Base class for all expressions. All other expressions should extend this class.
- [Class: BaseType<JSON>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/BaseType.md): Base class for all types. All other types should extend this class.
- [Class: BaseVariableField<VariableMeta>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/BaseVariableField.md): Variable Field abstract class, which is the base class for VariableDeclaration and Property VariableDeclaration is used to declare a variable in a block scope.Property is used to declare a property in an object.
- [Class: BooleanType](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/BooleanType.md): Represents a boolean type.
- [Class: CancellationTokenSource](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/CancellationTokenSource.md)
- [Class: Circle](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/Circle.md): The Circle object is used to help draw graphics and can also be used to specify a hit area for displayObjects.

- [Class: ContainerModule](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ContainerModule.md)
- [Class: ContextMenuService](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ContextMenuService.md): 圈选右键菜单相关 service
- [Class: CustomType](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/CustomType.md): Represents a custom type.
- [Class: DataNode<Data>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/DataNode.md): Represents a general data node with no child nodes.
- [Class: DefaultClipboardService](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/DefaultClipboardService.md): 剪贴板服务，一般用于管理临时的复制黏贴数据

- [Class: DisposableCollection](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/DisposableCollection.md): An object that performs a cleanup operation when .dispose() is called. Some examples of how disposables are used: An event listener that removes itself when .dispose() is called.The return value from registering a provider. When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

This is the V2 of KeyPathExpression, with the following improvements: returnType is copied to a new instance to avoid reference issues.Circular reference detection is introduced.

Represents a key path expression, which is used to reference a variable by its key path.

When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

- [Class: Scope<ScopeMeta>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/Scope.md): Represents a variable scope, which manages its own set of variables and their lifecycle. scope.output represents the variables declared within this scope.scope.available represents all variables accessible from this scope, including those from parent scopes.
- [Class: ScopeChain](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ScopeChain.md): Manages the dependency relationships between scopes.
This is an abstract class, and specific implementations determine how the scope order is managed.
- [Class: ScopeChainTransformService](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ScopeChainTransformService.md): A service for transforming scope chains.
- [Class: ScopeOutputData](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/ScopeOutputData.md): Manages the output variables of a scope.

- [Class: StringType](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/StringType.md): Base class for all types. All other types should extend this class.
- [Class: Computation<V>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/Tracker.Computation.md): Tracker.Computation A Computation object represents code that is repeatedly rerun
in response to
reactive data changes. Computations don't have return values; they just
perform actions, such as rerendering a template on the screen. Computations
are created using Tracker.autorun. Use stop to prevent further rerunning of a
computation.
- [Class: Dependency](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/Tracker.Dependency.md): Tracker.Dependency A Dependency represents an atomic unit of reactive data that a
computation might depend on. Reactive data sources such as Session or
Minimongo internally create different Dependency objects for different
pieces of data, each of which may be depended on by multiple computations.
When the data changes, the computations are invalidated.
- [Class: Transform](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/Transform.md): Transform that takes care about its versions Memberof PIXI
- [Class: TransformData](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/TransformData-1.md): Copyright (c) 2025 Bytedance Ltd. and/or its affiliates
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

- [Class: VariableDeclaration<VariableMeta>](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/VariableDeclaration.md): VariableDeclaration is a variable field that represents a variable declaration.
- [Class: VariableDeclarationList](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/VariableDeclarationList.md): An ASTNode represents a fundamental unit of variable information within the system's Abstract Syntax Tree.
It can model various constructs, for example: Declarations: const a = 1Expressions: a.b.cTypes: number, string, boolean Here is some characteristic of ASTNode: Tree-like Structure: ASTNodes can be nested to form a tree, representing complex variable structures.Extendable: New features can be added by extending the base ASTNode class.Reactive: Changes in an ASTNode's value trigger events, enabling reactive programming patterns.Serializable: ASTNodes can be converted to and from a JSON format (ASTNodeJSON) for storage or transmission.
- [Class: VariableEngine](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/VariableEngine.md): The core of the variable engine system.
It manages scopes, variables, and events within the system.
- [Class: VariableFieldKeyRenameService](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/classes/VariableFieldKeyRenameService.md): This service is responsible for detecting when a variable field's key is renamed.
It listens for changes in variable declaration lists and object properties, and
determines if a change constitutes a rename operation.

Type parameters Parameters Returns { kind: string  } & JSON The ASTNode JSON object.

- [Function: wrapDegrees](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/functions/Angle.wrapDegrees.md): Angle.wrapDegrees wrapDegrees(angle): number Wrap an angle in degrees. Wraps the angle to a value in the range of -180 to 180. Parameters Returns number The wrapped angle, in degrees.

- [Function: afterFlush](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/functions/Tracker.afterFlush.md): Tracker.afterFlush afterFlush(f): void Schedules a function to be called during the next flush, or later in the current flush if one is in progress, after all invalidated computations have been rerun.  The function will be run once and not on subsequent flushes unless afterFlush is called again. Parameters Returns void
- [Function: autorun](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/functions/Tracker.autorun.md): Tracker.autorun autorun<T>(f, options?): Computation<T> Run a function now and rerun it later whenever its dependencies
change. Returns a Computation object that can be used to stop or observe the
rerunning. Type parameters Parameters Returns Computation<T>

This matches Tracker.active unless we are inside Tracker.nonreactive, which nullfies currentComputation even though an enclosing computation may still be running. Returns boolean

- [Function: arrayUnion](/en/auto-docs/free-layout-editor/functions/arrayUnion.md): arrayUnion(arr): any[] Parameters Returns any[] See https://stackoverflow.com/a/9229821
export function arrayUnion(arr: any[]): any[] {
return [...new Set(arr)]
}

- [Interface: Disposable](/en/auto-docs/playground-react/interfaces/Disposable-1.md): An object that performs a cleanup operation when .dispose() is called. Some examples of how disposables are used: An event listener that removes itself when .dispose() is called.The return value from registering a provider. When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

- [Interface: I18nLanguage](/en/auto-docs/i18n/interfaces/I18nLanguage.md)
- [Variable: I18n](/en/auto-docs/i18n/variables/I18n.md): Const I18n: I18nImpl
- [Class: ReactiveBaseState<V>](/en/auto-docs/reactive/classes/ReactiveBaseState.md)
- [Class: ReactiveState<V>](/en/auto-docs/reactive/classes/ReactiveState.md)
- [Class: Computation<V>](/en/auto-docs/reactive/classes/Tracker.Computation.md): Tracker.Computation A Computation object represents code that is repeatedly rerun
in response to
reactive data changes. Computations don't have return values; they just
perform actions, such as rerendering a template on the screen. Computations
are created using Tracker.autorun. Use stop to prevent further rerunning of a
computation.
- [Class: Dependency](/en/auto-docs/reactive/classes/Tracker.Dependency.md): Tracker.Dependency A Dependency represents an atomic unit of reactive data that a
computation might depend on. Reactive data sources such as Session or
Minimongo internally create different Dependency objects for different
pieces of data, each of which may be depended on by multiple computations.
When the data changes, the computations are invalidated.
- [Function: afterFlush](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.afterFlush.md): Tracker.afterFlush afterFlush(f): void Schedules a function to be called during the next flush, or later in the current flush if one is in progress, after all invalidated computations have been rerun.  The function will be run once and not on subsequent flushes unless afterFlush is called again. Parameters Returns void
- [Function: autorun](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.autorun.md): Tracker.autorun autorun<T>(f, options?): Computation<T> Run a function now and rerun it later whenever its dependencies
change. Returns a Computation object that can be used to stop or observe the
rerunning. Type parameters Parameters Returns Computation<T>
- [Function: flush](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.flush.md): Tracker.flush flush(options?): void Process all reactive updates immediately and ensure that all invalidated computations are rerun. Parameters Returns void
- [Function: getCurrentComputation](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.getCurrentComputation.md): Tracker.getCurrentComputation getCurrentComputation(): Computation | undefined Returns Computation | undefined
- [Function: inFlush](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.inFlush.md): Tracker.inFlush inFlush(): boolean True if we are computing a computation now, either first time or recompute.  This matches Tracker.active unless we are inside Tracker.nonreactive, which nullfies currentComputation even though an enclosing computation may still be running. Returns boolean
- [Function: isActive](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.isActive.md): Tracker.isActive isActive(): boolean Returns boolean
- [Function: onInvalidate](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.onInvalidate.md): Tracker.onInvalidate onInvalidate(f): void Parameters Returns void
- [Function: withComputation](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.withComputation.md): Tracker.withComputation withComputation<T>(computation, f): T 函数在响应式模块中执行 Type parameters Parameters Returns T
- [Function: withoutComputation](/en/auto-docs/reactive/functions/Tracker.withoutComputation.md): Tracker.withoutComputation withoutComputation<T>(f): T 函数在非响应式模块中执行 Type parameters Parameters Returns T

- [Namespace: Tracker](/en/auto-docs/reactive/modules/Tracker.md): Tracker 是一套 响应式依赖追踪 库，来源于 Meteor.Tracker
https://docs.meteor.com/api/Tracker.html#tracker-autorun-and-async-callbacks

Some examples of how disposables are used: An event listener that removes itself when .dispose() is called.The return value from registering a provider. When .dispose() is called, the provider is unregistered.

- [Function: arrayUnion](/en/auto-docs/utils/functions/arrayUnion.md): arrayUnion(arr): any[] Parameters Returns any[] See https://stackoverflow.com/a/9229821
export function arrayUnion(arr: any[]): any[] {
return [...new Set(arr)]
}

- [Function: isASTMatchSchema](/en/auto-docs/form-antd-materials/functions/JsonSchemaUtils.isASTMatchSchema.md): JsonSchemaUtils.isASTMatchSchema isASTMatchSchema(typeAST, schema): boolean Check if the AST type is match the JSON Schema Parameters Returns boolean
- [Function: schemaToAST](/en/auto-docs/form-antd-materials/functions/JsonSchemaUtils.schemaToAST.md): JsonSchemaUtils.schemaToAST schemaToAST(jsonSchema): ASTNodeJSON | undefined Converts a JSON schema to an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) representation.
This function recursively processes the JSON schema and creates corresponding AST nodes. For more information on JSON Schema, refer to the official documentation:
https://json-schema.org/ Parameters Returns ASTNodeJSON | undefined An AST node representing the JSON schema, or undefined if the schema type is not recognized.

- [Function: formatLegacyRefOnInit](/en/auto-docs/form-antd-materials/functions/formatLegacyRefOnInit.md): formatLegacyRefOnInit(value): any In flowgram 0.2.0, for introducing Loop variable functionality,
the FlowRefValueSchema type definition is updated: interface LegacyFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string;
} interface NewFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string[];
} For making sure backend json will not be changed, we provide format legacy ref utils for updating the formData How to use: Call formatLegacyRefOnSubmit on the formData before submittingCall formatLegacyRefOnInit on the formData after submitting Example: formMeta: {
formatOnSubmit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnSubmit(data),
formatOnInit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnInit(data),
} Parameters Returns any
- [Function: formatLegacyRefOnSubmit](/en/auto-docs/form-antd-materials/functions/formatLegacyRefOnSubmit.md): formatLegacyRefOnSubmit(value): any In flowgram 0.2.0, for introducing Loop variable functionality,
the FlowRefValueSchema type definition is updated: interface LegacyFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string;
} interface NewFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string[];
} For making sure backend json will not be changed, we provide format legacy ref utils for updating the formData How to use: Call formatLegacyRefOnSubmit on the formData before submittingCall formatLegacyRefOnInit on the formData after submitting Example: formMeta: {
formatOnSubmit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnSubmit(data),
formatOnInit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnInit(data),
} Parameters Returns any

- [Function: isASTMatchSchema](/en/auto-docs/form-materials/functions/JsonSchemaUtils.isASTMatchSchema.md): JsonSchemaUtils.isASTMatchSchema isASTMatchSchema(typeAST, schema): boolean Check if the AST type is match the JSON Schema Parameters Returns boolean
- [Function: schemaToAST](/en/auto-docs/form-materials/functions/JsonSchemaUtils.schemaToAST.md): JsonSchemaUtils.schemaToAST schemaToAST(jsonSchema): ASTNodeJSON | undefined Converts a JSON schema to an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) representation.
This function recursively processes the JSON schema and creates corresponding AST nodes. For more information on JSON Schema, refer to the official documentation:
https://json-schema.org/ Parameters Returns ASTNodeJSON | undefined An AST node representing the JSON schema, or undefined if the schema type is not recognized.

- [Function: createInjectMaterial](/en/auto-docs/form-materials/functions/createInjectMaterial.md): createInjectMaterial<Props>(Component, params?): WithRenderKey<React.FC<Props>> Creates a material component wrapper with dependency injection support This Higher-Order Component (HOC) implements a dynamic component replacement mechanism
for material components. It automatically checks if a custom renderer is registered
in the editor context, using the injected component if available, otherwise
falling back to the default component. Type parameters Parameters Returns WithRenderKey<React.FC<Props>> Wrapper component with dependency injection support Example Description Data flow explanation: Register components to FlowRendererRegistry in use-editor-propsInjectMaterial reads renderers from FlowRendererRegistryIf registered renderer exists and type is REACT, use injected componentIf not exists or type mismatch, fallback to default component

- [Function: formatLegacyRefOnInit](/en/auto-docs/form-materials/functions/formatLegacyRefOnInit.md): formatLegacyRefOnInit(value): any In flowgram 0.2.0, for introducing Loop variable functionality,
the FlowRefValueSchema type definition is updated: interface LegacyFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string;
} interface NewFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string[];
} For making sure backend json will not be changed, we provide format legacy ref utils for updating the formData How to use: Call formatLegacyRefOnSubmit on the formData before submittingCall formatLegacyRefOnInit on the formData after submitting Example: formMeta: {
formatOnSubmit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnSubmit(data),
formatOnInit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnInit(data),
} Parameters Returns any
- [Function: formatLegacyRefOnSubmit](/en/auto-docs/form-materials/functions/formatLegacyRefOnSubmit.md): formatLegacyRefOnSubmit(value): any In flowgram 0.2.0, for introducing Loop variable functionality,
the FlowRefValueSchema type definition is updated: interface LegacyFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string;
} interface NewFlowRefValueSchema {
type: 'ref';
content: string[];
} For making sure backend json will not be changed, we provide format legacy ref utils for updating the formData How to use: Call formatLegacyRefOnSubmit on the formData before submittingCall formatLegacyRefOnInit on the formData after submitting Example: formMeta: {
formatOnSubmit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnSubmit(data),
formatOnInit: (data) => formatLegacyRefOnInit(data),
} Parameters Returns any

- [Function: listenRefSchemaChange](/en/auto-docs/form-materials/functions/listenRefSchemaChange.md): listenRefSchemaChange(cb): EffectOptions[] Example:
const formMeta = {
effect: {
'inputsValues.*': listenRefSchemaChange(({ name, schema, form }) => {
form.setValueIn(${name}.schema, schema);
})
}
} Parameters Returns EffectOptions[]
- [Function: listenRefValueChange](/en/auto-docs/form-materials/functions/listenRefValueChange.md): listenRefValueChange(cb): EffectOptions[] Example:
const formMeta = {
effect: {
'inputsValues.*': listenRefValueChange(({ name, variable, form }) => {
const schema = JsonSchemaUtils.astToSchema(variable?.type);
form.setValueIn(${name}.schema, schema);
})
}
} Parameters Returns EffectOptions[]

paths  whoes value is undefined. Parameters Returns string[]

Parameters Returns string[]

该方法默认 isMatchOrParent(pattern, path) 为 true, 不做为false 的错误处理。 Parameters Returns string
